description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline with stages build, package, and deploy, using the docker:latest image, the docker:dind service, and a node_modules cache keyed by ${CI_COMMIT_REF_SLUG}. In the build stage add a manual build-and-deploy-to-aws job (tags docker) that runs apk add --no-cache curl jq python3 py-pip, pip install awscli, docker info, echoes CI variables, logs into AWS ECR with $(aws ecr get-login ...), builds the Docker image $AWS_CONTAINER_REGISTRY_URL:$CI_COMMIT_SHA from Dockerfile.flightchainapi, tags it with the same SHA and latest, and pushes it, only on the master branch. In the deploy stage add manual deploy-airline-or-airport and deploy-docs-to-elasticbeanstalk jobs (image coxauto/aws-ebcli, tags docker) that echo CI variables, show EB version, copy and edit Dockerrun.template.aws.json to set the AWS_DOCKER_CONTAINER_IMAGE_TAG, add and cat the file, then run eb deploy to $AWS_EB_ENV_NAME and to FlightChainAPI-docs respectively, only on master. Also in the deploy stage add manual k8s-deploy-ba-dev, k8s-deploy-ba-prod, k8s-deploy-lhr-dev, k8s-deploy-lhr-prod, k8s-deploy-mia-dev, k8s-deploy-mia-prod, k8s-deploy-mk-dev, k8s-deploy-mu-dev, k8s-deploy-qantas-dev, and k8s-deploy-swissport-dev jobs (image google/cloud-sdk) that cd into gitlab-k8s, chmod +x k8s-deploy.sh, execute ./k8s-deploy.sh, and set environment‑specific variables for certificates, identity, keys, service name and signing identity, each manual where indicated. Finally, in the package stage add a docker-build job (tags shared) that lists files, logs into the GitLab registry, builds the Docker image with Dockerfile.flightchainapi using build args PROJECT and VERSION, tags it as ${CI_REGISTRY_IMAGE}:${VERSION}.${SHORT_SHA}, and pushes it.
